posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
• 2005 Domaine Bernard Defaix Chablis 1er Cru Côte de Léchet - France, Burgundy, Chablis, Chablis 1er Cru (6/3/2020)
    A great nose of smoky mineral, white mushroom and citrus. It is rich and unctuous in the mouth but also light on its feet. There’s so much saline goodness and pulpy citrus fruits a plenty. It has good drive and decent cut and is long and clean. Drinking beautifully.

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l
posts.cooked: Great note. This was recently released here. A steal at $60 CAD. I’ve had two and loved it each time.
posts.display_username: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l

posts.name: Vincent Fritzsche
posts.cooked: Thanks! I have a bottle of this and one of Vaillons in the queue.

posts.name: Doug Ackerman
posts.cooked: These Defaix wines are showstoppers. at least the 05s we’ve had. Opened the 2005 Domaine Bernard Defaix Chablis 1er Cru Côte de Léchet last week and blown away. Also the Les Lys is fantastic. I have some Grenouilles stashed away but at 15 yrs old will start to open them too. I can’t think of a better Chablis outside of the stalwart Ravenaus and Dauvissats, but those stratospheric prices are deal breakers for me.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Definitely Bernard, not Daniel-Etienne? I only ask as the latter Defaix late-released a lot of 2005s lately, whereas Bernard’s are long gone from the market. And they have the same appellations…

posts.name: Marshall Manning
posts.cooked: If Doug and Vincent got them recently in PDX I’m pretty sure those were the Daniel-Etienne Defaix wines. But I know both of them have cellars, too!

posts.name: Vincent Fritzsche
posts.cooked: My bad, yes D-E Defaix in my case. Shoulda caught that one…

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
Definitely Bernard, not Daniel-Etienne? I only ask as the latter Defaix late-released a lot of 2005s lately, whereas Bernard’s are long gone from the market. And they have the same appellations…

Well, for Daniel-Etienne, his late-release is his current release!

I also like his wines a lot (especially at the price), though they tend to be a slightly rounder type of Chablis, proably in part because they’re always released with so much age.

posts.name: Marshall Manning
posts.cooked:
My bad, yes D-E Defaix in my case. Shoulda caught that one…

I Defaix you to find a better Chablis for the money.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I also like his wines a lot (especially at the price), though they tend to be a slightly rounder type of Chablis, proably in part because they’re always released with so much age.

That along with unusually long sur lie élevage…

posts.name: Doug Ackerman
posts.cooked: Whoops yes Daniel-Etienne for me too. I blame Vincent for missing this important information.

posts.name: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l
posts.display_username: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l

posts.name: David Henry
posts.cooked: Last night, I opened the 2014 Daniel-Etienne Defaix/Domaine du Vieux Chateau Chablis Vieilles Vignes. Surprising depth and intensity for a village level Chablis, drinking very well, exhibiting noteworthy classic Chablis character (seashell, seaweed, marine life, etc.) with the significant level of balancing acidity of the fine 2014 vintage in the region. This is one of the best village level Chablis I have had in recent years, and I enjoyed it more than the 2005 Daniel-Etienne Defaix Chablis Cote de Lechet I recently opened.
